message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending task, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT change logic, only migrate structure
- The source of truth is card-place, consult it only if in doubt
- The code has already been copied to this repository
- Everything must be migrated to the new architecture/Style Guide (./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)

WORKFLOW:
1. Read progress.txt and identify the most important pending single task
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there is a task:
   a. Update task status to "in progress" in progress.txt
   b. Execute task following STYLE_GUIDE.md
   c. Launch 3 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. Output status block (see below)
   i. Stop

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For multiple lint/typescript/test errors, fix just one error and register others in progress.txt to be fixed in the next iteration
7. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
8. Never change tasks (unless to change status), deprecate and create new ones.
9. Task statuses:
    " " (or "TODO");
    "DONE";
    "STARTED";
    "DEPRECATED"

   141→## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED
   142→
   143→### 2.1 Create src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts ✅ DONE
   144→File created at src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts with all required functionality.
   145→
   146→### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
   147→Added: `export * from './usePoints';`
   148→
   149→This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.
   150→
   151→```typescript
   152→import { useCallback } from 'react';
   153→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
   154→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   155→import {
   156→  getPointsProducts,
   157→  clearPointsProducts,
   158→  getPointsBalance,
   159→  getParameterPointsProgram,
   160→} from '../services';
   161→import {
   162→  PointsProductsStatus,
   163→  PointsBalanceStatus,
   164→  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
   165→} from '../models';
   166→
   167→export function usePoints() {
   168→  // Products from products store
   169→  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
   170→  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();
   171→
   172→  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
   173→  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));
   174→
   175→  // Parameterization from parameter store
   176→  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
   177→    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
   178→  );
   179→
   180→  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
   181→    await Promise.all([
   182→      getPointsBalance(),
   183→      getPointsProducts(),
   184→      getParameterPointsProgram(),
   185→    ]);
   186→  }, []);
   187→
   188→  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
   189→    await getPointsBalance();
   190→  }, []);
   191→
   192→  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
   193→    await getPointsProducts();
   194→  }, []);
   195→
   196→  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
   197→    await getParameterPointsProgram();
   198→  }, []);
   199→
   200→  const clear = useCallback(() => {
   201→    clearPointsProducts();
   202→  }, []);
   203→
   204→  return {
   205→    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
   206→    points: pointsBalance.points,
   207→    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
   208→    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,
   209→
   210→    // Products (from products store)
   211→    products: pointsProducts.products,
   212→    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
   213→    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,
   214→
   215→    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
   216→    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
   217→    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
   218→    isLoadingParameterization:
   219→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
   220→    isErrorParameterization:
   221→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
   222→
   223→    // Actions
   224→    requestPointsBalance,
   225→    requestPointsProducts,
   226→    requestParameterization,
   227→    requestInitialData,
   228→    clear,
   229→  };
   230→}
   231→```

   256→import { useCallback } from 'react';
   257→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
   258→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   259→import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
   260→import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';
   261→
   262→export function useMarketplace() {
   263→  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));
   264→
   265→  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
   266→    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
   267→      await getMarketplace(params);
   268→    },
   269→    []
   270→  );
   271→
   272→  const clear = useCallback(() => {
   273→    clearMarketplace();
   274→  }, []);
   275→
   276→  return {
   277→    data: marketplace.data,
   278→    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
   279→    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
   280→    requestGetMarketplace,
   281→    clear,
   282→  };
   283→}
   284→```

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (VERIFIED 2026-01-26)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure: COMPLETE

================================================================================

================================================================================

## pointsReducer (Redux) → Multiple Zustand stores

| Redux Field                    | Zustand Location                              | Service Function           |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------|

| isErrorBalance                 | pointsBalance.status === ERROR                | -                          |
| products                       | pointsProducts.products (products store)      | getPointsProducts()        |
| isLoadingProducts              | pointsProducts.status === LOADING             | -                          |

| conversionFactor               | parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor       | -                          |

| isErrorParameterization        | parameterPointsProgram.status === ERROR       | -                          |

| appliedExpiringFilters         | pointsExtractFilters.appliedExpiringFilters   | -                          |

## rescueReducer (Redux) → rescue store (Zustand)

|-------------|-----------------------------|------------------|
| isLoading   | rescue.status === LOADING   | performRescue()  |
| isError     | rescue.status === ERROR     | -                |
| status      | rescue.responseStatus       | -                |

## getMarketplaceReducer (Redux) → marketplace store (Zustand)

|-------------|-------------------------------|-------------------|

| isError     | marketplace.status === ERROR  | -                 |
| data        | marketplace.data              | -                 |

## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 2.1 Create src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts with all required functionality.

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
Added: `export * from './usePoints';`

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

### 2.2 Verify ParameterPointsProgramStatus exists
File: src/services/models/parameter.model.ts

Should have:
```typescript
export enum ParameterPointsProgramStatus {
  IDLE = 'idle',
  LOADING = 'loading',
  SUCCESS = 'success',
  ERROR = 'error',
}
```

If missing, add it.

## Phase 3: Create useMarketplace Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 3.1 Create src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts with all required functionality.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 14 hooks analyzed (including nested hooks from usePoints and useScreenViewTracking). Key findings: (1) useEffect([requestParameterization]) fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton, referentially stable; getParameterPointsProgram service has LOADING/SUCCESS guard preventing re-entry; store transitions (IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS) cause bounded 3 re-renders but never re-trigger the effect. (2) Second useFocusEffect with [setPointsValue, setReaisValue] deps is safe — React guarantees useState setters are referentially stable, making this equivalent to useCallback([]); state resets fire on focus/blur only (not on re-renders) and no effect depends on pointsValue/reaisValue/pointsError/reaisError. (3) First useFocusEffect with [] deps + hasFocused ref guard — fires analytics once per focus, no state mutations. (4) useScreenViewTracking internal useEffect([isFocused]) — fires on boolean primitive toggle, analytics only, no state mutations, eslint-disable on dispatchEventFocus is intentional. (5) useMemo([conversionFactor]) — depends on route params (frozen per navigation entry). Non-blocking observations: invoiceList() creates new array every render (could be useMemo'd), Intl.NumberFormat instantiated every render, 8 unmemoized handler functions, Dimensions.get('window') called every render instead of useWindowDimensions(), duplicate analytics on focus (useScreenViewTracking V2 + useFocusEffect V3 — may be intentional dual-system). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict with HIGH confidence.)

12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
================================================================================

These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).

### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. The if(isFocused) guard provides a second layer preventing execution on blur. 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict. No corrections needed.)

(2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 7 hooks analyzed: usePoints() (custom hook with 3 useShallow selectors + 5 useCallback([]) singletons — all referentially stable), useState(0) refresh with no setter (constant 0, dead code), useRef(false) hasFocused (ref guard for analytics), useFocusEffect(useCallback(fn, [])) (stable callback, fires once per focus with ref guard, no state mutations), useNavigation() (stable context ref), useCallback(requestAll, [requestParameterization, requestPointsProducts]) (both deps are useCallback([]) singletons — frozen), useEffect([requestParameterization, refresh]) (fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton and refresh is constant 0; three-layer defense: stable deps + immutable refresh + service LOADING/SUCCESS guard). Store transitions via useShallow cause bounded re-renders (max 3: IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS/ERROR) that never re-trigger the effect. Non-issues noted: dead refresh state (vestigial), invoiceList() called twice per render (minor perf), non-memoized handlers (child not React.memo), no useEffect cleanup for async (safe with Zustand external state). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict.)

(3) First useFocusEffect with [] deps + hasFocused ref guard — fires analytics once per focus, no state mutations. /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   138→
   139→```
   140→atomic/atoms/Button/
   141→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143→└── Models/
   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```
   146→
   147→**Models/index.d.ts**
   148→
   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {
   151→  text: string;
   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {
   156→  pressed: boolean;
   157→}
   158→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159→  handlePress: () => void;
   160→}
   161→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162→```
   163→
   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```
   185→
   186→**view.tsx (render)**
   187→
   188→```tsx
   189→import React from 'react';
   190→import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191→import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192→import { useButton } from './index';
   193→import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194→
   195→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197→
   198→  return (
   199→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200→      <View>
   201→        <Text>
   202→          {props.text}
   203→          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
   204→        </Text>
   205→      </View>
   206→    </Pressable>
   207→  );
   208→}
   209→```
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Project Organization
   214→
   215→```
   216→src/
   217→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   218→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220→│   ├── atoms/
   221→│   ├── molecules/
   222→│   └── organisms/
   223→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   228→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   231→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   236→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   238→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240→```
   241→
   242→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```
   325→
   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```
   370→
   371→- For Use on Class.
   372→
   373→```ts
   374→class Auth {
   375→  private readonly setAuth;
   376→
   377→  constructor () {
   378→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  function doSomething(data) {
   382→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→```
   386→
   387→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   389→
   390→```ts
   391→const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392→const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393→```
   394→
   395→---
   396→
   397→## Routes Naming
   398→
   399→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   400→
   401→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   402→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   403→
   404→---
   405→
   406→## Folder Naming Rules
   407→
   408→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
   409→| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
   410→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
   411→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
   412→
   413→---
   414→
   415→## Code Standards
   416→
   417→**Language**: English only.  
   418→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   419→
   420→**Explanatory variables**
   421→
   422→```js
   423→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   424→const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
   425→  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   426→const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
   427→isNumber(1);
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Semantic constants**
   431→
   432→```js
   433→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   434→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   435→```
   436→
   437→**Library workarounds with link**
   438→
   439→```js
   440→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   441→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   442→event.target.value = '';
   443→```
   444→
   445→**JSDoc**
   446→
   447→```js
   448→/**
   449→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   450→ * @param {*} eventName
   451→ * @param {*} maxLength
   452→ */
   453→```
   454→
   455→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→useEffect(() => {
   459→  (async () => {
   460→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   461→    setSum(42);
   462→  })();
   463→}, [setSum]);
   464→```
   465→
   466→**Extract conditions**
   467→
   468→```ts
   469→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   470→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   471→```
   472→
   473→**Optional chaining**
   474→
   475→```ts
   476→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   477→```
   478→
   479→**JSX indentation**
   480→
   481→```tsx
   482→{
   483→  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
   484→}
   485→```
   486→
   487→**Early returns**
   488→
   489→```ts
   490→if (!data) return null;
   491→return <View />;
   492→```
   493→
   494→**Nullish coalescing**
   495→
   496→```ts
   497→return data ?? 0;
   498→```
   499→
   500→---
   501→
   502→## Styles Patterns
   503→
   504→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   505→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   506→
   507→---
   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   554→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   555→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   556→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   557→
   558→---
   559→
   560→## Checklist
   561→
   562→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   563→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   564→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   565→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   566→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   567→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   568→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   569→
   570→---
   571→
   572→Happy shipping! 🚀
   573→

    39→        leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}

    48→      </BasicScreen>
    49→    );

    51→
    52→  if (isLoading && !transaction) {
    53→    return (
    54→      <BasicScreen
    55→        title="Detalhes do pedido"
    56→        leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
    57→        hasScroll
    58→        errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
    59→      >
    60→        <Box flex={1} justify="center" align="center" minHeight={400}>
    61→          <ActivityIndicator size="large" color={theme.colors.BRAND_BASE_01} />
    62→        </Box>
    63→      </BasicScreen>
    64→    );
    65→  }
    66→
    67→  if (!transaction) {
    68→    return null;
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  const renderDivider = () => {
    72→    return (
    73→      <SeparatorHorizontal
    74→        borderWidth="smaller"
    75→        borderColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02"
    76→        height={1}
    77→        width="100%"
    78→        mt="xxxxs"
    79→      />

    81→  };
    82→
    83→  const renderMarketplaceContent = () => {

    85→
    86→    return (
    87→      <>
    88→        <Text type="paragraphXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="xxxxs" mt="xxxxs">

    90→        </Text>
    91→        <Text type="paragraphMD" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03">
    92→          #{marketplaceContent.orderNumber}
    93→        </Text>
    94→        {marketplaceContent.status && (
    95→          <>
    96→            {renderDivider()}
    97→            <Text type="paragraphXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="xxxxs" mt="xxxxs">
    98→              Status do pedido
    99→            </Text>
   100→            <TagLabel
   101→              type={(marketplaceContent.statusColor as any) || 'neutral'}
   102→              text={marketplaceContent.status}
   103→              showSpacing
   104→            />
   105→          </>
   106→        )}
   107→        {marketplaceContent.paymentType && (
   108→          <>
   109→            {renderDivider()}
   110→            <Text type="paragraphXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="xxxxs" mt="xxxxs">
   111→              Forma de pagamento

1→import { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→
     3→import {
     4→  AirlineMilesSlice,
     5→  createAirlineMilesSlice,
     6→  createMarketplaceSlice,
     7→  createParameterPointsProgramSlice,
     8→  createPermissionSlice,
     9→  createPointsExtractSlice,
    10→  createPointsProductsSlice,
    11→  createRescueSlice,
    12→  MarketplaceSlice,
    13→  ParameterPointsProgramSlice,
    14→  PermissionSlice,
    15→  PointsExtractSlice,
    16→  PointsProductsSlice,
    17→  RescueSlice,
    18→} from '../index';
    19→
    20→export type RewardsProgramRootStore = ParameterPointsProgramSlice &
    21→  PermissionSlice &
    22→  AirlineMilesSlice &
    23→  PointsExtractSlice &
    24→  PointsProductsSlice &
    25→  RescueSlice &
    26→  MarketplaceSlice;
    27→
    28→type Slice<T> = StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>;
    29→const asSlice = <T>(slice: StateCreator<RewardsProgramRootStore, [], [], T>) =>
    30→  slice as Slice<T>;
    31→
    32→export function setRewardsProgramSlices<T extends RewardsProgramRootStore>(
    33→  ...a: Parameters<Slice<T>>
    34→) {
    35→  return {
    36→    ...asSlice<ParameterPointsProgramSlice>(createParameterPointsProgramSlice)(
    37→      ...a,
    38→    ),
    39→    ...asSlice<PermissionSlice>(createPermissionSlice)(...a),
    40→    ...asSlice<AirlineMilesSlice>(createAirlineMilesSlice)(...a),
    41→    ...asSlice<PointsExtractSlice>(createPointsExtractSlice)(...a),
    42→    ...asSlice<PointsProductsSlice>(createPointsProductsSlice)(...a),
    43→    ...asSlice<RescueSlice>(createRescueSlice)(...a),
    44→    ...asSlice<MarketplaceSlice>(createMarketplaceSlice)(...a),
    45→  };
    46→}
    47→

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
================================================================================

These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).

### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

Animated.Value.setValue()/stopAnimation()/loop().start() operate imperatively outside React reconciliation. 3 review agents: 2 approved, 1 flagged missing useEffect cleanup as resource leak on unmount — PRE-EXISTING code quality issue, not an infinite loop, documented below as future task.)

- [DONE] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/atomic/templates/BasicScreen/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 5 hooks analyzed: useNavigation (stable context ref), useNavigationState (returns primitive string — stable by value), useCallback(nativeGoBackHandler, [hasNativeGoBack, nativeGoBackFn]) (both deps are primitives or external; if parent doesn't memoize nativeGoBackFn, causes BackHandler effect churn but NOT a loop since addEventListener/remove are native bridge calls with no React state mutation), useEffect([props.hasError]) fires eventModalView which is analytics-only with no state side effects (eslint-disable on currentRouteName is intentional — including it would re-fire analytics on navigation while error persists), useEffect([nativeGoBackHandler]) manages BackHandler listener imperatively with cleanup — no state feedback path. handleGoToHome is not memoized but only passed to pure FC BasicScreenView (no loop since child re-render doesn't feed back). 3 review agents: all APPROVED. Observations (non-blocking): unstable nativeGoBackFn from parent causes unnecessary native re-subscriptions, handleGoToHome could be useCallback-wrapped if View were ever React.memo'd.)

- [DONE] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/hooks/tracking/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. # Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (VERIFIED 2026-01-26)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure: COMPLETE

| extract      | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | ✅         | ✅      | -      |

================================================================================
STATE MAPPING: Redux → Zustand
================================================================================

## pointsReducer (Redux) → Multiple Zustand stores

| Redux Field                    | Zustand Location                              | Service Function           |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------|
| points                         | pointsBalance.points (extract store)          | getPointsBalance()         |
| isLoadingBalance               | pointsBalance.status === LOADING              | -                          |
| isErrorBalance                 | pointsBalance.status === ERROR                | -                          |
| products                       | pointsProducts.products (products store)      | getPointsProducts()        |
| isLoadingProducts              | pointsProducts.status === LOADING             | -                          |
| isErrorProducts                | pointsProducts.status === ERROR               | -                          |
| pointsQuantity                 | parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity         | getParameterPointsProgram()|
| conversionFactor               | parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor       | -                          |
| isLoadingParameterization      | parameterPointsProgram.status === LOADING     | -                          |
| isErrorParameterization        | parameterPointsProgram.status === ERROR       | -                          |
| appliedTransactionsFilters     | pointsExtractFilters.appliedTransactionsFilters | -                        |
| appliedExpiringFilters         | pointsExtractFilters.appliedExpiringFilters   | -                          |

## rescueReducer (Redux) → rescue store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location            | Service Function |
|-------------|-----------------------------|------------------|
| isLoading   | rescue.status === LOADING   | performRescue()  |
| isError     | rescue.status === ERROR     | -                |
| status      | rescue.responseStatus       | -                |

## getMarketplaceReducer (Redux) → marketplace store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location              | Service Function  |
|-------------|-------------------------------|-------------------|
| isLoading   | marketplace.status === LOADING| getMarketplace()  |
| isError     | marketplace.status === ERROR  | -                 |
| data        | marketplace.data              | -                 |

## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 2.1 Create src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts ✅ DONE

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
Added: `export * from './usePoints';`

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

### 2.2 Verify ParameterPointsProgramStatus exists
File: src/services/models/parameter.model.ts

Should have:
```typescript

  IDLE = 'idle',
  LOADING = 'loading',
  SUCCESS = 'success',
  ERROR = 'error',
}
```

If missing, add it.

## Phase 3: Create useMarketplace Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 3.1 Create src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts with all required functionality.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

12.1 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND (PRE-EXISTING - NOT CAUSED BY MIGRATION)
================================================================================

These issues existed in the original card-place codebase. They are NOT migration errors but should be documented for future reference. Per migration rules, these should NOT be fixed during migration (no logic changes allowed).

### 12.1.1 rescue.service.ts - Response Validation Logic (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/rescue.service.ts:23
Issue: `if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message)` uses OR instead of AND
Impact: Any response with a message object could be treated as success
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.2 InvoiceCashbackSummary - campaingId Typo (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx:97
Issue: Route param named `campaingId` (misspelled) but payload field is `campaignId` (correct)
Impact: Field name mismatch could cause data loss if caller uses correct spelling
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.3 InvoiceCashbackOnboarding - pointsQuantity Always 0 (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx:51, parameter.store.ts:26
Issue: pointsQuantity initialized to 0 and never updated (setPointsQuantity never called)
Impact: Display shows "Cada 0 pontos valem R$ 0.00" always
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.4 InvoiceCashbackPoints - Dual conversionFactor Sources (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/InvoiceCashbackPoints/index.tsx:54
Issue: Uses route.params.conversionFactor (defaults to 0) instead of usePoints() hook value
Impact: If route param missing, all conversions silently fail (multiply by 0)
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.5 ExtractRewardProgram - Pagination Race Condition (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/index.ts:285-287
Issue: handleScroll uses refs instead of reactive state, allowing multiple concurrent requests
Impact: Duplicate API requests, data merging bugs, page counter desync
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.6 ExtractRewardProgram - Data Grouping Lost on Append (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:97
Issue: appendExtractData overwrites groupedTransactions with only new page's groups
Impact: Date grouping destroyed on pagination, UI shows incomplete grouped data
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.7 ExtractRewardProgram - "A expirar" Tab Empty (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/screens/ExtractRewardProgram/view.tsx:176-180
Issue: Second tab renders empty fragment <></>
Impact: Tab is non-functional
Status: PRE-EXISTING - do not fix during migration

### 12.1.8 parameter.store.ts - Missing Error Field (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/parameter.store.ts:22-28, 8-15
Issue: No error field in state, no setError method in actions
Impact: Parameter fetch errors cannot be stored or displayed
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design inconsistency from original architecture

### 12.1.9 extract.store.ts - setBalanceStatus Doesn't Clear Error (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/store/extract.store.ts:120-123
Issue: setBalanceStatus doesn't clear error on LOADING (unlike setExtractStatus)
Impact: Stale error messages remain visible on retry
Status: PRE-EXISTING - inconsistency within same store

### 12.1.10 products.service.ts - shouldFetchProducts Missing SUCCESS Guard (PRE-EXISTING)
Location: src/services/services/products.service.ts:7-10
Issue: Only checks LOADING status, not SUCCESS - allows refetch of cached data
Impact: Unnecessary refetching, no cache respect
Status: PRE-EXISTING - design decision, not a bug

(2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 5 hooks analyzed: useNavigation (stable context ref), useNavigationState (returns primitive string — stable by value), useCallback(nativeGoBackHandler, [hasNativeGoBack, nativeGoBackFn]) (both deps are primitives or external; if parent doesn't memoize nativeGoBackFn, causes BackHandler effect churn but NOT a loop since addEventListener/remove are native bridge calls with no React state mutation), useEffect([props.hasError]) fires eventModalView which is analytics-only with no state side effects (eslint-disable on currentRouteName is intentional — including it would re-fire analytics on navigation while error persists), useEffect([nativeGoBackHandler]) manages BackHandler listener imperatively with cleanup — no state feedback path. handleGoToHome is not memoized but only passed to pure FC BasicScreenView (no loop since child re-render doesn't feed back). 3 review agents: all APPROVED. Observations (non-blocking): unstable nativeGoBackFn from parent causes unnecessary native re-subscriptions, handleGoToHome could be useCallback-wrapped if View were ever React.memo'd.)

- [DONE] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/hooks/tracking/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. Single useEffect with [isFocused] dependency — a primitive boolean from React Navigation's focus/blur events. Effect only fires on actual focus transitions (true<->false). dispatchEventFocus is an inline closure capturing route, analytic, title, handleAfterDispatch — intentionally excluded from deps via eslint-disable (adding it would fire analytics on every render). Stale closure is benign: all 5 callers pass static string literals, and values are fresh at focus transitions since React re-renders before running effects. handleAfterDispatch is not passed by any current caller; even if it called setState in parent, isFocused would remain unchanged so the effect would not re-fire. The if(isFocused) guard provides a second layer preventing execution on blur. 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict. No corrections needed.)

- [DONE] Deeply and recursively review the possible react hooks inside "src/screens/InvoiceCashbackOnboarding/index.tsx" against possible infinite loops. (2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 7 hooks analyzed: usePoints() (custom hook with 3 useShallow selectors + 5 useCallback([]) singletons — all referentially stable), useState(0) refresh with no setter (constant 0, dead code), useRef(false) hasFocused (ref guard for analytics), useFocusEffect(useCallback(fn, [])) (stable callback, fires once per focus with ref guard, no state mutations), useNavigation() (stable context ref), useCallback(requestAll, [requestParameterization, requestPointsProducts]) (both deps are useCallback([]) singletons — frozen), useEffect([requestParameterization, refresh]) (fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton and refresh is constant 0; three-layer defense: stable deps + immutable refresh + service LOADING/SUCCESS guard). Store transitions via useShallow cause bounded re-renders (max 3: IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS/ERROR) that never re-trigger the effect. Non-issues noted: dead refresh state (vestigial), invoiceList() called twice per render (minor perf), non-memoized handlers (child not React.memo), no useEffect cleanup for async (safe with Zustand external state). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict.)

(2026-01-28: NO INFINITE LOOP RISK. 14 hooks analyzed (including nested hooks from usePoints and useScreenViewTracking). Key findings: (1) useEffect([requestParameterization]) fires exactly once on mount — requestParameterization is useCallback([]) singleton, referentially stable; getParameterPointsProgram service has LOADING/SUCCESS guard preventing re-entry; store transitions (IDLE→LOADING→SUCCESS) cause bounded 3 re-renders but never re-trigger the effect. (2) Second useFocusEffect with [setPointsValue, setReaisValue] deps is safe — React guarantees useState setters are referentially stable, making this equivalent to useCallback([]); state resets fire on focus/blur only (not on re-renders) and no effect depends on pointsValue/reaisValue/pointsError/reaisError. (3) First useFocusEffect with [] deps + hasFocused ref guard — fires analytics once per focus, no state mutations. (4) useScreenViewTracking internal useEffect([isFocused]) — fires on boolean primitive toggle, analytics only, no state mutations, eslint-disable on dispatchEventFocus is intentional. (5) useMemo([conversionFactor]) — depends on route params (frozen per navigation entry). Non-blocking observations: invoiceList() creates new array every render (could be useMemo'd), Intl.NumberFormat instantiated every render, 8 unmemoized handler functions, Dimensions.get('window') called every render instead of useWindowDimensions(), duplicate analytics on focus (useScreenViewTracking V2 + useFocusEffect V3 — may be intentional dual-system). 3 review agents: all APPROVED, unanimous SAFE verdict with HIGH confidence.)

#### 1. CRITICAL: Missing `origination` field in PointsTransaction AND extract.repository.ts mapping

**Problem**: The consolidated tasks mention adding `origination?: string` to `PointsTransaction` in `extract.model.ts`, but there's a deeper issue:

- In card-place, `TransactionDto` in `extract.api.ts` has `origination?: string`
- However, `getTransactionFromDto()` in `extract.repository.ts` does NOT map this field to `PointsTransaction`
- The view accesses `item.origination` from `myOrders` which comes from `pointsExtract.extractData.transactions`

**In progress.txt MCP32275_01 states**:
```
Add: marketplace?: Marketplace to PointsTransaction interface
```

**Missing**: The task does NOT mention:
1. Adding `origination?: string` to `PointsTransaction` (though this is mentioned in PLAN_C section but NOT in consolidated MCP32275_01)
2. Updating `extract.repository.ts` to map `origination` from DTO to model

**Fix**: MCP32275_01 should explicitly state:
```
Add: MarketplaceItem, MarketplaceOrder, MarketplacePayment, Marketplace interfaces
Add: marketplace?: Marketplace to PointsTransaction interface
Add: origination?: string to PointsTransaction interface
```

```

```

```

This assumes `item.origination` exists, but without MCP32275_01 correctly adding `origination` to `PointsTransaction` AND updating the repository mapping, this will cause a TypeScript error.

#### 4. Import path inconsistency for MyOrdersDetails/Models/index.ts

**In MCP32275_10**:

```

**Actual card-place code**:

```

## REVIEW VERDICT: **NEEDS_CORRECTION**

### Summary

The MCP004 task consolidation into MCP32275_01 through MCP32275_21 is largely comprehensive, but there are specific issues that need correction.

---

### Issues Found

#### 1. CRITICAL: Missing `origination` field in PointsTransaction AND extract.repository.ts mapping

**Problem**: The consolidated tasks mention adding `origination?: string` to `PointsTransaction` in `extract.model.ts`, but there's a deeper issue:

- In card-place, `TransactionDto` in `extract.api.ts` has `origination?: string`
- However, `getTransactionFromDto()` in `extract.repository.ts` does NOT map this field to `PointsTransaction`
- The view accesses `item.origination` from `myOrders` which comes from `pointsExtract.extractData.transactions`

**In progress.txt MCP32275_01 states**:
```
Add: marketplace?: Marketplace to PointsTransaction interface
```

**Missing**: The task does NOT mention:
1. Adding `origination?: string` to `PointsTransaction` (though this is mentioned in PLAN_C section but NOT in consolidated MCP32275_01)
2. Updating `extract.repository.ts` to map `origination` from DTO to model

**Fix**: MCP32275_01 should explicitly state:
```
Add: MarketplaceItem, MarketplaceOrder, MarketplacePayment, Marketplace interfaces
Add: marketplace?: Marketplace to PointsTransaction interface
Add: origination?: string to PointsTransaction interface
```

And add a new task or extend MCP32275_01:
```
Update extract.repository.ts: Add origination mapping in getTransactionFromDto()
```

---

#### 2. Missing screens/index.ts export

**Problem**: PLAN_B explicitly mentions:
```
- [ ] P2.7.1 Update src/screens/index.ts (export MyOrdersDetails) <-- NEW
```

**In consolidated tasks**: MCP32275_07 only mentions:
```
Add to: models/index.ts, api/index.ts, repository/index.ts, services/index.ts, store/index.ts
```

**Missing**: `src/screens/index.ts` is not mentioned. If screens are exported from an index file, this needs to be updated.

**Verification needed**: Check if `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/screens/index.ts` exists and exports screens.

---

#### 3. Task MCP32275_18 uses `item.origination` without verifying field exists

**Problem**: The task states:
```
Change: onGhostButtonPress={() => handleDetailsOrders(item.id, item.type, item.icon, item.status, item.origination)}
```

This assumes `item.origination` exists, but without MCP32275_01 correctly adding `origination` to `PointsTransaction` AND updating the repository mapping, this will cause a TypeScript error.

---

#### 4. Import path inconsistency for MyOrdersDetails/Models/index.ts

**In MCP32275_10**:
```
Fix imports: '../../../services/models' NOT '../../../store/rewardProgram/models'
```

**Actual card-place code**:
```typescript
import { PointsTransaction } from '../../../store/rewardProgram/models';
```

**Rewardprogram structure**: The services are in `src/services/`, so the correct import should be:
```typescript
import { PointsTransaction } from '../../../services/models';
```

This is correctly identified, but the task should verify the exact relative path depth (3 levels up from `screens/MyOrdersDetails/Models/`).

---

#### 5. Routes update dependency not explicit

**MCP32275_14 and MCP32275_15** update routes, but the task order suggests MCP32275_12 (Create MyOrdersDetails/index.tsx) happens first. This is correct since the screen must exist before routes import it.

**However**, MCP32275_15 says:
```
Import and add: <Stack.Screen name="MyOrdersDetails" component={MyOrdersDetails} />
```

But in rewardprogram, the export structure is `FintechCardPlaceRoutes` is a fragment, not a navigator. Need to verify the exact integration point.

---

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (VERIFIED 2026-01-26)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure: COMPLETE

================================================================================
STATE MAPPING: Redux → Zustand
================================================================================

## pointsReducer (Redux) → Multiple Zustand stores

| Redux Field                    | Zustand Location                              | Service Function           |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------|
| points                         | pointsBalance.points (extract store)          | getPointsBalance()         |
| isLoadingBalance               | pointsBalance.status === LOADING              | -                          |
| isErrorBalance                 | pointsBalance.status === ERROR                | -                          |
| products                       | pointsProducts.products (products store)      | getPointsProducts()        |
| isLoadingProducts              | pointsProducts.status === LOADING             | -                          |
| isErrorProducts                | pointsProducts.status === ERROR               | -                          |
| pointsQuantity                 | parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity         | getParameterPointsProgram()|
| conversionFactor               | parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor       | -                          |
| isLoadingParameterization      | parameterPointsProgram.status === LOADING     | -                          |
| isErrorParameterization        | parameterPointsProgram.status === ERROR       | -                          |
| appliedTransactionsFilters     | pointsExtractFilters.appliedTransactionsFilters | -                        |
| appliedExpiringFilters         | pointsExtractFilters.appliedExpiringFilters   | -                          |

## rescueReducer (Redux) → rescue store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location            | Service Function |
|-------------|-----------------------------|------------------|
| isLoading   | rescue.status === LOADING   | performRescue()  |
| isError     | rescue.status === ERROR     | -                |
| status      | rescue.responseStatus       | -                |

## getMarketplaceReducer (Redux) → marketplace store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location              | Service Function  |
|-------------|-------------------------------|-------------------|
| isLoading   | marketplace.status === LOADING| getMarketplace()  |
| isError     | marketplace.status === ERROR  | -                 |
| data        | marketplace.data              | -                 |
